            V-253304 - The built-in Microsoft password complexity filter must be enabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253304r1051045_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AC-000040
            Identities:CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
            CCI-000192 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (a); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (a))
            Description: The use of complex passwords increases their strength against guessing and brute-force attacks. This setting configures the system to verify that newly created passwords conform to the Windows password complexity policy.
            Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
            Run "gpedit.msc".

            Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy.

            If the value for "Password must meet complexity requirements" is not set to "Enabled", this is a finding.

            If the site is using a password filter that requires this setting be set to "Disabled" for the filter to be used, this would not be considered a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy >> "Password must meet complexity requirements" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253304
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AC-000040 - The built-in Microsoft password complexity filter must be enabled.
            Description:The use of complex passwords increases their strength against guessing and brute-force attacks. This setting configures the system to verify that newly created passwords conform to the Windows password complexity policy.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Password must meet complexity requirements' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330400 (passwordpolicy_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Password must meet complexity requirements' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000002 (passwordpolicy_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330400 (passwordpolicy_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'password_complexity' must be equal to '1'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • max_passwd_age equals '3628800'
            • min_passwd_age equals '0'
            • min_passwd_len equals '0'
            • password_hist_len equals '0'
            • password_complexity equals '0'
            • reversible_encryption equals '0'
            • anonymous_name_lookup equals '0'
            Result Analysis:password_complexity = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

            V-253306 - The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253306r991570_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000005
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Account Logon >> Credential Validation - Failure
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Logon >> "Audit Credential Validation" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253306
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000005 - The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Failure')
                • false ('Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330600 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Failure'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330600 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment:'Credential Validation' is set to 'Failure'
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'credential_validation' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • application_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • computer_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • distribution_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • other_account_management_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • security_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • user_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • dpapi_activity equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • process_creation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • process_termination equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • rpc_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • directory_service_access equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • directory_service_changes equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • detailed_directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

            • ipsec_main_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

            V-253307 - The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253307r991570_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000010
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Account Logon >> Credential Validation - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Logon >> "Audit Credential Validation" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253307
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000010 - The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Success')
                • false ('Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
            Tests:

            V-253308 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - Security Group Management successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253308r971541_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000030
            Identities:CCI-001914 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 (3); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 (3))
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Security Group Management records events such as creating, deleting or changing of security groups, including changes in group members.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Account Management >> Security Group Management - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Management >> "Audit Security Group Management" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253308
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000030 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - Security Group Management successes.

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Security Group Management records events such as creating, deleting or changing of security groups, including changes in group members.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Audit Security Group Management' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')

            V-253309 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253309r958566_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000035
            Identities:CCI-001314 (NIST SP 800-53: SI-11 c; NIST SP 800-53A: SI-11.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SI-11 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SI-11 b)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Account Management >> User Account Management - Failure
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Management >> "Audit User Account Management" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253309
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000035 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Failure')
                • false ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false

            V-253310 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253310r991551_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000040
            Identities:CCI-001403 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Account Management >> User Account Management - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Management >> "Audit User Account Management" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253310
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000040 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Success')
                • false ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
            Tests:

            V-253311 - The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253311r1051047_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000045
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            CCI-003938 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-5 (1) (b))
            CCI-001814 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-5 (1))
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Plug and Play activity records events related to the successful connection of external devices.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Detailed Tracking >> Plug and Play Events - Success
            Fix Text:Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Detailed Tracking >> "Audit PNP Activity" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253311
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000045 - The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Plug and Play activity records events related to the successful connection of external devices.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Audit - Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity - Success Only)
                • false (Audit - Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity - Success and Failure)

            V-253312 - The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Process Creation successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253312r1051048_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000050
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            CCI-003938 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-5 (1) (b))
            CCI-001814 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-5 (1))
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Process creation records events related to the creation of a process and the source.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Detailed Tracking >> Process Creation - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Detailed Tracking >> "Audit Process Creation" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253312
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000050 - The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Process Creation successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Process creation records events related to the creation of a process and the source.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Audit - Process Creation - Success only)
                • false (Audit - Process Creation - Success and Failure)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25331200 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Audit - Process Creation - Success only
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

            Object Comment:

            • Collect any available items.

            V-253313 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Account Lockout failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253313r991578_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000054
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Account Lockout events can be used to identify potentially malicious logon attempts.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:

            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").

            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Logon/Logoff >> Account Lockout - Failure
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Account Lockout" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253313
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000054 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Account Lockout failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Account Lockout events can be used to identify potentially malicious logon attempts.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit Account Lockout' is set to 'Failure')
                • false ('Audit Account Lockout' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
            Tests:

            V-253314 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253314r991570_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000060
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Group Membership records information related to the group membership of a user's logon token.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings (Windows Vista or later) to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Logon/Logoff >> Group Membership - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Group Membership" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253314
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000060 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Group Membership records information related to the group membership of a user's logon token.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Audit - Logon/Logoff - Group Membership - Success Only)
                • false (Audit - Logon/Logoff - Group Membership - Success and Failure)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25331400 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Audit - Logon/Logoff - Group Membership - Success Only
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25331400 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment:'Audit Group Membership' is set to 'Success'
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'group_membership' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

            V-253315 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Logoff successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253315r958406_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000065
            Identities:CCI-000067 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (1).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (1))
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Logoff records user logoffs. If this is an interactive logoff, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Logon/Logoff >> Logoff - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Logoff" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253315
            Definition Result:false

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Logoff records user logoffs. If this is an interactive logoff, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:

              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))

                • false (Audit - Logoff - Success and Failure)

            V-253318 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Special Logon successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253318r991578_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000080
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Special Logon records special logons which have administrative privileges and can be used to elevate processes.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings (Windows Vista or later) to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Logon/Logoff >> Special Logon - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Special Logon" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Special Logon records special logons which have administrative privileges and can be used to elevate processes.

            V-253319 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - File Share failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253319r991572_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000081
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Auditing file shares records events related to connection to shares on a system including system shares such as C$.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings (Windows Vista or later) to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:

            Open PowerShell or a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"
            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following:
            Object Access >> File Share - Failure

            If the system does not audit the above, this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit File Share" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253319
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000081 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - File Share failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Auditing file shares records events related to connection to shares on a system including system shares such as C$.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit File Share' is set to 'Failure')
                • false ('Audit File Share' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25331900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)

            Object Comment:

            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Auditing file shares records events related to connection to shares on a system including system shares such as C$.

            If the system does not audit the above, this is a finding.

            V-253321 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - Other Object Access Events successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253321r991572_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000083
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Auditing for other object access records events related to the management of task scheduler jobs and COM+ objects.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.
            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:

            Open PowerShell or a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").

            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following:

            Object Access >> Other Object Access Events - Success

            If the system does not audit the above, this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Other Object Access Events" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253321
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000083 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - Other Object Access Events successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Auditing for other object access records events related to the management of task scheduler jobs and COM+ objects.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit Other Object Access Events' is set to 'Success')
                • false ('Audit Other Object Access Events' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25332100 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Audit Other Object Access Events' is set to 'Success'

            V-253322 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - Other Object Access Events failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253322r991572_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000084
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Auditing for other object access records events related to the management of task scheduler jobs and COM+ objects.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:

            Open PowerShell or a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"
            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following:
            Object Access >> Other Object Access Events - Failure

            If the system does not audit the above, this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Other Object Access Events" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253322
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000084 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - Other Object Access Events failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Auditing for other object access records events related to the management of task scheduler jobs and COM+ objects.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit Other Object Access Events' is set to 'Failure')
                • false ('Audit Other Object Access Events' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')

            V-253323 - The system must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253323r991583_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000085
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Auditing object access for removable media records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Object Access >> Removable Storage - Failure

            Some virtual machines may generate excessive audit events for access to the virtual hard disk itself when this setting is enabled. This may be set to Not Configured in such cases and would not be a finding. This must be documented with the ISSO to include mitigations such as monitoring or restricting any actual removable storage connected to the VM.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Removable Storage" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253323
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000085 - The system must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Auditing object access for removable media records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Audit - Object Access - Removable Storage - Failure Only)
                • false (Audit - Object Access - Removable Storage - Success and Failure)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25332300 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)

            Object Comment:

            • Collect any available items.

            V-253324 - The system must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253324r991583_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000090
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Auditing object access for removable media records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings (Windows Vista or later) to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Object Access >> Removable Storage - Success

            Some virtual machines may generate excessive audit events for access to the virtual hard disk itself when this setting is enabled. This may be set to Not Configured in such cases and would not be a finding. This must be documented with the ISSO to include mitigations such as monitoring or restricting any actual removable storage connected to the VM.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Removable Storage" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253324
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000090 - The system must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Auditing object access for removable media records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Audit - Object Access - Removable Storage - Success Only)
                • false (Audit - Object Access - Removable Storage - Success and Failure)

            V-253325 - The system must be configured to audit Policy Change - Audit Policy Change successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253325r991572_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000100
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Policy Change records events related to changes in audit policy.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Policy Change >> Audit Policy Change - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> "Audit Policy Change" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253325

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Policy Change records events related to changes in audit policy.

            V-253326 - The system must be configured to audit Policy Change - Authentication Policy Change successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253326r991572_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000105
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Authentication Policy Change records events related to changes in authentication policy including Kerberos policy and Trust changes.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.
            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Policy Change >> Authentication Policy Change - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> "Audit Authentication Policy Change" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253326
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000105 - The system must be configured to audit Policy Change - Authentication Policy Change successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Authentication Policy Change records events related to changes in authentication policy including Kerberos policy and Trust changes.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Audit Authentication Policy Change' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25332600 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Audit Authentication Policy Change' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25332600 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'authentication_policy_change' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

            V-253327 - The system must be configured to audit Policy Change - Authorization Policy Change successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253327r991572_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000107
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Authorization Policy Change records events related to changes in user rights, such as create a token object.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            -Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            -Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding.

            Policy Change >> Authorization Policy Change - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> "Audit Authorization Policy Change" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:

            Authorization Policy Change records events related to changes in user rights, such as create a token object.

            V-253328 - The system must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253328r958732_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000110
            Identities:CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Privilege Use >> Sensitive Privilege Use - Failure
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Privilege Use >> "Audit Sensitive Privilege Use" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253328
            Definition Result:false

            Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253329
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000115 - The system must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25332900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25332900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sensitive_privilege_use' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25332901 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sensitive_privilege_use' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

            • account_lockout equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

            • ipsec_main_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • ipsec_quick_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • logoff equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • logon equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

            • filtering_platform_packet_drop equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

            • authorization_policy_change equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

            • mpssvc_rule_level_policy_change equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • other_policy_change_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • non_sensitive_privilege_use equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • other_privilege_use_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • sensitive_privilege_use equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • ipsec_driver equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • other_system_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • security_state_change equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • security_system_extension equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • system_integrity equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • group_membership equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
