            V-253330 - The system must be configured to audit System - IPsec Driver failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253330r991586_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000120
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            IPsec Driver records events related to the IPsec Driver such as dropped packets.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            System >> IPsec Driver - Failure
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit IPsec Driver" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253330
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000120 - The system must be configured to audit System - IPsec Driver failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            IPsec Driver records events related to the IPsec Driver such as dropped packets.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Audit - IPSec Driver - Failure only)
                • false (Audit - IPSec Driver - Success and Failure)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Audit - IPSec Driver - Failure only
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.

            State Comment:Audit - IPSec Driver - Failure only

            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'ipsec_driver' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'

            V-253331 - The system must be configured to audit System - Other System Events successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253331r991579_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000130
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Other System Events records information related to cryptographic key operations and the Windows Firewall service.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            System >> Other System Events - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit Other System Events" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253331
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000130 - The system must be configured to audit System - Other System Events successes.

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Other System Events records information related to cryptographic key operations and the Windows Firewall service.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Other System Events' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')
            Tests:

            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.

            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_system_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'

            V-253333 - The system must be configured to audit System - Security State Change successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253333r991575_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000140
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Security State Change records events related to changes in the security state, such as startup and shutdown of the system.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            System >> Security State Change - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit Security State Change" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253333
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000140 - The system must be configured to audit System - Security State Change successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Security State Change records events related to changes in the security state, such as startup and shutdown of the system.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Audit - Security State Change - Success only)
                • false (Audit - Security State Change - Success and Failure)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333300 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Audit - Security State Change - Success only
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333300 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment:Audit - Security State Change - Success only
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'security_state_change' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

            V-253334 - The system must be configured to audit System - Security System Extension successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253334r991575_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000150
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Security System Extension records events related to extension code being loaded by the security subsystem.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            System >> Security System Extension - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit Security System Extension" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253334
            Definition Result:false

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Security System Extension records events related to extension code being loaded by the security subsystem.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))

                • false ('Audit Security System Extension' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')

            V-253335 - The system must be configured to audit System - System Integrity failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253335r991573_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000155
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            System Integrity records events related to violations of integrity to the security subsystem.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            System >> System Integrity - Failure
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit System Integrity" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253335
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000155 - The system must be configured to audit System - System Integrity failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            System Integrity records events related to violations of integrity to the security subsystem.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Audit - System Integrity - Failure only)
                • false (Audit - System Integrity - Success and Failure)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333500 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Audit - System Integrity - Failure only
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333500 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment:Audit - System Integrity - Failure only
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'system_integrity' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'

            V-253336 - The system must be configured to audit System - System Integrity successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253336r991573_rule

            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            System Integrity records events related to violations of integrity to the security subsystem.
            Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

            Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            System >> System Integrity - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit System Integrity" with "Success" selected.

            V-253337 - The Application event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253337r958752_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000500
            Identities:CCI-001849 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-4)
            Description: Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
            Check Text:If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this is NA. This must be documented with the ISSO.

            If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application\

            Value Name: MaxSize

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00008000 (32768) (or greater)
            Fix Text:If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this is NA. This must be documented with the ISSO.

            Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Event Log Service >> Application >> "Specify the maximum log file size (KB)" to "Enabled" with a "Maximum Log Size (KB)" of "32768" or greater.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253337
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000500 - The Application event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.
            Description:Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (The Application event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333700 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:The Application event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25333700 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application'
            • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333700 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '32768'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application\MaxSize'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253338 - The security event log size must be configured to a value that holds at least one week's worth of audit records.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253338r1186375_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000505
            Identities:CCI-001849 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-4)
            Description: Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel. System administrators (SAs) must monitor the volume of security event audit records and increase the registry value for the log size as needed.
            Check Text:If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this requirement is not applicable. This must be documented with the information system security officer (ISSO).

            The registry configuration setting below must be set (at least) to a value equal to the size needed to contain one week's worth of audit records in the security event log. The value used below is an example that assumes a typical week’s log size of 5GB.

            If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Note: The following registry entry is an example; the value must equal at least one week's worth of records.

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Security\

            Value Name: MaxSize

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value:0x49960800 (5120000) (or greater)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Event Log Service >> Security >> "Specify the maximum log file size (KB)" to "Enabled" with a "Maximum Log Size (KB)" of a value that will contain one week of audit records or greater.

            If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this must be documented with the ISSO.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253338
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000505 - The security event log size must be configured to a value that holds at least one week's worth of audit records.
            Description:nadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel. System administrators (SAs) must monitor the volume of security event audit records and increase the registry value for the log size as needed.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (The Security event log size is configured to 5120000 KB or greater)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333800 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:The Security event log size is configured to 5120000 KB or greater
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25333800 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Security'
            • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333800 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '5120000'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Security\MaxSize'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253339 - The System event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253339r958752_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000510
            Identities:CCI-001849 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-4)
            Description: Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\System\

            Value Type: REG_DWORD

            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\System'
            • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333700 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '32768'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\System\MaxSize'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253344 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Other Policy Change Events Failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253344r958412_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000555
            Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Other Policy Change Events contains events about EFS Data Recovery Agent policy changes, changes in Windows Filtering Platform filter, status on Security policy settings updates for local Group Policy settings, Central Access Policy changes, and detailed troubleshooting events for Cryptographic Next Generation (CNG) operations.
            Check Text:Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Policy Change >> Other Policy Change Events - Failure
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change>> "Audit Other Policy Change Events" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253344
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000555 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Other Policy Change Events Failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Other Policy Change Events contains events about EFS Data Recovery Agent policy changes, changes in Windows Filtering Platform filter, status on Security policy settings updates for local Group Policy settings, Central Access Policy changes, and detailed troubleshooting events for Cryptographic Next Generation (CNG) operations.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Other Policy Change Events' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334400 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Other Policy Change Events' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334400 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_policy_change_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334401 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_policy_change_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

            [ false ]

            V-253345 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit other Logon/Logoff Events Successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253345r958412_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000560
            Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events determines whether Windows generates audit events for other logon or logoff events. Logon events are essential to understanding user activity and detecting potential attacks.
            Check Text:Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Logon/Logoff >> Other Logon/Logoff Events - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253345
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000560 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit other Logon/Logoff Events Successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events determines whether Windows generates audit events for other logon or logoff events. Logon events are essential to understanding user activity and detecting potential attacks.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Audit Logon' is set to 'Failure' and 'Succss and Failure)

            Title:'Audit Logon' is set to 'Failure' and 'Succss and Failure
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334500 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment:'Log On' is set to 'Success'
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_logon_logoff_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334501 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)

            V-253347 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Detailed File Share Failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253347r958412_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000570
            Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Detailed File Share allows the user to audit attempts to access files and folders on a shared folder.
            The Detailed File Share setting logs an event every time a file or folder is accessed, whereas the File Share setting only records one event for any connection established between a client and file share. Detailed File Share audit events include detailed information about the permissions or other criteria used to grant or deny access.
            Check Text:Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Object Access >> Detailed File Share - Failure
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit Detailed File Share" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253347
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000570 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Detailed File Share Failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit Detailed File Share allows the user to audit attempts to access files and folders on a shared folder.
            The Detailed File Share setting logs an event every time a file or folder is accessed, whereas the File Share setting only records one event for any connection established between a client and file share. Detailed File Share audit events include detailed information about the permissions or other criteria used to grant or deny access.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Detailed File Share' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Detailed File Share' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'detailed_file_share' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334701 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment:

            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'detailed_file_share' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

            V-253348 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change Successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253348r958412_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000575
            Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change determines whether the operating system generates audit events when changes are made to policy rules for the Microsoft Protection Service (MPSSVC.exe).
            Check Text:Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Policy Change >> MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change - Success
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253348
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000575 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change Successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change determines whether the operating system generates audit events when changes are made to policy rules for the Microsoft Protection Service (MPSSVC.exe).
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334800 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334800 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment:'MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change' is set to 'Success'
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'mpssvc_rule_level_policy_change' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334801 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment:'MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure'
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'mpssvc_rule_level_policy_change' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

            V-253349 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change Failures.

            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change determines whether the operating system generates audit events when changes are made to policy rules for the Microsoft Protection Service (MPSSVC.exe).
            Check Text:Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
            Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
            Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

            Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

            Policy Change >> MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change - Failure
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:

            V-253352 - The display of slide shows on the lock screen must be disabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253352r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000010
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: Slide shows that are displayed on the lock screen could display sensitive information to unauthorized personnel. Turning off this feature will limit access to the information to a logged on user.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\

            Value Name: NoLockScreenSlideshow

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Control Panel >> Personalization >> "Prevent enabling lock screen slide show" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253352
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000010 - The display of slide shows on the lock screen must be disabled.
            Description:Slide shows that are displayed on the lock screen could display sensitive information to unauthorized personnel. Turning off this feature will limit access to the information to a logged on user.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (Verifies 'Prevent enabling lock screen slide show' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335200 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Verifies 'Prevent enabling lock screen slide show' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335200 (registry_object)
            Object Comment:Registry object for HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreenSlideshow
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoLockScreenSlideshow'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreenSlideshow'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253353 - IPv6 source routing must be configured to highest protection.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253353r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000020
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Configuring the system to disable IPv6 source routing protects against spoofing.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters\

            Value Name: DisableIpSourceRouting

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 2
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting IPv6) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)" to "Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled".

            This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253353
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000020 - IPv6 source routing must be configured to highest protection.
            Description:Configuring the system to disable IPv6 source routing protects against spoofing.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting IPv6) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)' is set to 'Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335300 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting IPv6) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)' is set to 'Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335300 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableIPSourceRouting'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters\DisableIPSourceRouting'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253354 - The system must be configured to prevent IP source routing.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253354r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000025
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Configuring the system to disable IP source routing protects against spoofing.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\

            Value Name: DisableIPSourceRouting

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 2
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)" to "Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled".

            This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253354
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000025 - The system must be configured to prevent IP source routing.
            Description:Configuring the system to disable IP source routing protects against spoofing.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)' is set to 'Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335400 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)' is set to 'Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335400 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableIPSourceRouting'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\DisableIPSourceRouting'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253358 - WDigest Authentication must be disabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253358r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000038
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: When the WDigest Authentication protocol is enabled, plain text passwords are stored in the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS) exposing them to theft. WDigest is disabled by default in Windows 11. This setting ensures this is enforced.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\Wdigest\

            Value Name: UseLogonCredential

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000000 (0)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "WDigest Authentication (disabling may require KB2871997)" to "Disabled".

            The patch referenced in the policy title is not required for Windows 11.

            This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and "SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253358
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000038 - WDigest Authentication must be disabled.
            Description:When the WDigest Authentication protocol is enabled, plain text passwords are stored in the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS) exposing them to theft. WDigest is disabled by default in Windows 11. This setting ensures this is enforced.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (WDigest Authentication is disabled)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335800 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:WDigest Authentication is disabled
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335800 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\Wdigest'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'UseLogonCredential'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\Wdigest\UseLogonCredential'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253359 - Run as different user must be removed from context menus.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253359r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000039
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: The "Run as different user" selection from context menus allows the use of credentials other than the currently logged on user. Using privileged credentials in a standard user session can expose those credentials to theft. Removing this option from context menus helps prevent this from occurring.
            Check Text:If the following registry values do not exist or are not configured as specified, this is a finding.
            The policy configures the same Value Name, Type and Value under four different registry paths.

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Paths:
            \SOFTWARE\Classes\batfile\shell\runasuser\
            \SOFTWARE\Classes\cmdfile\shell\runasuser\
            \SOFTWARE\Classes\exefile\shell\runasuser\
            \SOFTWARE\Classes\mscfile\shell\runasuser\

            Value Name: SuppressionPolicy

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00001000 (4096)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "Remove "Run as Different User" from context menus" to "Enabled".

            This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and "SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253359
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000039 - Run as different user must be removed from context menus.
            Description:The "Run as different user" selection from context menus allows the use of credentials other than the currently logged on user. Using privileged credentials in a standard user session can expose those credentials to theft. Removing this option from context menus helps prevent this from occurring.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (batfile))
                • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (cmdfile))
                • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (exefile))
                • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (mscfile))
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335900 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (batfile)
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335900 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Classes\batfile\shell\runasuser'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SuppressionPolicy'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25335900 (registry_state)
            State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 4096
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '4096'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Classes\batfile\shell\runasuser\SuppressionPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335901 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (cmdfile)
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335901 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Classes\cmdfile\shell\runasuser'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SuppressionPolicy'

            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Classes\cmdfile\shell\runasuser\SuppressionPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335902 (registry_test)
