            V-253360 - Insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253360r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000040
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Insecure guest logons allow unauthenticated access to shared folders. Shared resources on a system must require authentication to establish proper access.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation\

            Value Name: AllowInsecureGuestAuth

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000000 (0)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Lanman Workstation >> "Enable insecure guest logons" to "Disabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253360
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000040 - Insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled.
            Description:Insecure guest logons allow unauthenticated access to shared folders. Shared resources on a system must require authentication to establish proper access.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Enable insecure guest logons' is set to 'Disabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336000 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Enable insecure guest logons' is set to 'Disabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336000 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowInsecureGuestAuth'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation\AllowInsecureGuestAuth'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253361 - Internet connection sharing must be disabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253361r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000044
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: Internet connection sharing makes it possible for an existing internet connection, such as through wireless, to be shared and used by other systems essentially creating a mobile hotspot. This exposes the system sharing the connection to others with potentially malicious purpose.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Network Connections\

            Value Name: NC_ShowSharedAccessUI

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000000 (0)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Network Connections >> "Prohibit use of Internet Connection Sharing on your DNS domain network" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253361
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000044 - Internet connection sharing must be disabled.
            Description:Internet connection sharing makes it possible for an existing internet connection, such as through wireless, to be shared and used by other systems essentially creating a mobile hotspot. This exposes the system sharing the connection to others with potentially malicious purpose.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Prohibit use of Internet Connection Sharing on your DNS domain network' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336100 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Prohibit use of Internet Connection Sharing on your DNS domain network' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336100 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Network Connections'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NC_ShowSharedAccessUI'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Network Connections\NC_ShowSharedAccessUI'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253363 - Windows 11 must be configured to prioritize ECC Curves with longer key lengths first.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253363r971535_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000052
            Identities:CCI-000803 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53A: IA-7.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-7)
            Description: Use of weak or untested encryption algorithms undermines the purposes of utilizing encryption to protect data. By default Windows uses ECC curves with shorter key lengths first. Requiring ECC curves with longer key lengths to be prioritized first helps ensure more secure algorithms are used.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Cryptography\Configuration\SSL\00010002\

            Value Name: EccCurves

            Value Type: REG_MULTI_SZ
            Value: NistP384 NistP256
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> SSL Configuration Settings >> "ECC Curve Order" to "Enabled" with "ECC Curve Order:" including the following in the order listed:

            NistP384
            NistP256
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253363
            Definition Result:false
            Title:Windows 11 must be configured to prioritize ECC Curves with longer key lengths first.
            Description:Use of weak or untested encryption algorithms undermines the purposes of utilizing encryption to protect data. By default Windows uses ECC curves with shorter key lengths first. Requiring ECC curves with longer key lengths to be prioritized first helps ensure more secure algorithms are used.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Prioritize ECC Curves with longer key lengths first. NistP384 Requirement)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25336301 (variable_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Prioritize ECC Curves with longer key lengths first. NistP384 Requirement
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25336301 (variable_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'var_ref' must be equal to 'oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:var:25336301'
            State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25336301 (variable_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern '^NistP384\s*NistP256$'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • var_ref equals 'oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:var:25336301'
            • value equals ''
            Result Analysis:'' failed test of 'pattern match' compared to '^NistP384\s*NistP256$'

            V-253365 - Connections to non-domain networks when connected to a domain authenticated network must be blocked.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253365r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000060
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Multiple network connections can provide additional attack vectors to a system and must be limited. When connected to a domain, communication must go through the domain connection.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WcmSvc\GroupPolicy\

            Value Name: fBlockNonDomain

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Windows Connection Manager >> "Prohibit connection to non-domain networks when connected to domain authenticated network" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253365
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000060 - Connections to non-domain networks when connected to a domain authenticated network must be blocked.
            Description:Multiple network connections can provide additional attack vectors to a system and must be limited. When connected to a domain, communication must go through the domain connection.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Prohibit connection to non-domain networks when connected to domain authenticated network' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336500 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Prohibit connection to non-domain networks when connected to domain authenticated network' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336500 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WcmSvc\GroupPolicy\'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'fBlockNonDomain'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WcmSvc\GroupPolicy\\fBlockNonDomain'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253366 - Wi-Fi Sense must be disabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253366r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000065
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Wi-Fi Sense automatically connects the system to known hotspots and networks that contacts have shared. It also allows the sharing of the system's known networks to contacts. Automatically connecting to hotspots and shared networks can expose a system to unsecured or potentially malicious systems.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WcmSvc\wifinetworkmanager\config\

            Value Name: AutoConnectAllowedOEM

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000000 (0)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> WLAN Service >> WLAN Settings>> "Allow Windows to automatically connect to suggested open hotspots, to networks shared by contacts, and to hotspots offering paid services" to "Disabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253366
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000065 - Wi-Fi Sense must be disabled.
            Description:Wi-Fi Sense automatically connects the system to known hotspots and networks that contacts have shared. It also allows the sharing of the system's known networks to contacts. Automatically connecting to hotspots and shared networks can expose a system to unsecured or potentially malicious systems.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (Verifies 'Allow Windows to automatically connect to suggested open hotspots, to networks shared by contacts, and to hotspots offering paid service' is set to 'Disabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336600 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Verifies 'Allow Windows to automatically connect to suggested open hotspots, to networks shared by contacts, and to hotspots offering paid service' is set to 'Disabled'

            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

            Object Comment:AutoConnectAllowedOEM
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WcmSvc\wifinetworkmanager\config'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AutoConnectAllowedOEM'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)

            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'

            V-253367 - Command line data must be included in process creation events.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253367r958422_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000066
            Identities:CCI-000135 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 (1))
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Enabling "Include command line data for process creation events" will record the command line information with the process creation events in the log. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Audit\

            Value Name: ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Audit Process Creation >> "Include command line in process creation events" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253367
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000066 - Command line data must be included in process creation events.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Enabling "Include command line data for process creation events" will record the command line information with the process creation events in the log. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Include command line in process creation events' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336700 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Include command line in process creation events' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336700 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Audit'
            • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Audit\ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253368 - Windows 11 must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253368r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000068
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: An exportable version of credentials is provided to remote hosts when using credential delegation which exposes them to theft on the remote host. Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard allow delegation of non-exportable credentials providing additional protection of the credentials. Enabling this configures the host to support Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation\

            Value Name: AllowProtectedCreds

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000001 (1)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Credentials Delegation >> "Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253368
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000068 - Windows 11 must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials.
            Description:An exportable version of credentials is provided to remote hosts when using credential delegation which exposes them to theft on the remote host. Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard allow delegation of non-exportable credentials providing additional protection of the credentials. Enabling this configures the host to support Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336800 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336800 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowProtectedCreds'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation\AllowProtectedCreds'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253369 - Virtualization-based Security must be enabled on Windows 11 with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253369r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000070
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Virtualization-based Security (VBS) provides the platform for the additional security features, Credential Guard and virtualization-based protection of code integrity. Secure Boot is the minimum security level with DMA protection providing additional memory protection. DMA Protection requires a CPU that supports input/output memory management unit (IOMMU).
            Check Text:Confirm virtualization-based Security is enabled and running with Secure Boot or Secure Boot and DMA Protection.

            For those devices that support virtualization-based security (VBS) features, including Credential Guard or protection of code integrity, this must be enabled. If the system meets the hardware and firmware dependencies for enabling VBS but it is not enabled, this is a CAT III finding.

            Virtualization-based security, including Credential Guard, currently cannot be implemented in virtual desktop implementations (VDI) due to specific supporting requirements including a TPM, UEFI with Secure Boot, and the capability to run the Hyper-V feature within the virtual desktop.

            For VDIs where the virtual desktop instance is deleted or refreshed upon logoff, this is NA.

            Run "PowerShell" with elevated privileges (run as administrator).

            Enter the following:

            "Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_DeviceGuard -Namespace root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard"

            If "RequiredSecurityProperties" does not include a value of "2" indicating "Secure Boot" (e.g., "{1, 2}"), this is a finding.

            If "Secure Boot and DMA Protection" is configured, "3" will also be displayed in the results (e.g., "{1, 2, 3}").

            If "VirtualizationBasedSecurityStatus" is not a value of "2" indicating "Running", this is a finding.

            Alternately:

            Run "System Information".

            Under "System Summary", verify the following:

            If "Device Guard virtualization-based security" does not display "Running", this is finding.

            If "Device Guard Required Security Properties" does not display "Base Virtualization Support, Secure Boot", this is finding.

            If "Secure Boot and DMA Protection" is configured, "DMA Protection" will also be displayed (e.g., "Base Virtualization Support, Secure Boot, DMA Protection").

            The policy settings referenced in the Fix section will configure the following registry values. However due to hardware requirements, the registry values alone do not ensure proper function.

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard\

            Value Name: EnableVirtualizationBasedSecurity
            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1

            Value Name: RequirePlatformSecurityFeatures
            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1 (Secure Boot only) or 3 (Secure Boot and DMA Protection)

            A Microsoft article on Credential Guard system requirement can be found at the following link:

            https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/itpro/windows/keep-secure/credential-guard-requirements
            Fix Text:Virtualization-based security, including Credential Guard, currently cannot be implemented in virtual desktop implementations (VDI) due to specific supporting requirements including a TPM, UEFI with Secure Boot, and the capability to run the Hyper-V feature within the virtual desktop.

            For VDIs where the virtual desktop instance is deleted or refreshed upon logoff, this is NA.

            Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Device Guard >> "Turn On virtualization-based Security" to "Enabled" with "Secure Boot" or "Secure Boot and DMA Protection" selected for "Select Platform Security Level:".

            A Microsoft article on Credential Guard system requirement can be found at the following link.
            https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/itpro/windows/keep-secure/credential-guard-requirements
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253369
            Definition Result:false
            Title:Virtualization-based Security must be enabled on Windows 11 with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection.
            Description:Virtualization-based Security must be enabled with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (RequiredSecurityProperties should return a value of 2)
              • false (VirtualizationBasedSecurityStatus should return a value of 2)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25336901 (cmdlet_test)
            Result:false
            Title:RequiredSecurityProperties should return a value of 2
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25336901 (cmdlet_object)
            Object Comment:DeviceGuard requiredsecurityproperties
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'module_name' must be equal to 'CimCmdlets'
            • check = 'verb' must be equal to 'Get'
            • check = 'noun' must be equal to 'CimInstance'
            • for 'parameters' the following must be true:
              • classname must be equal to 'Win32_DeviceGuard'
              • namespace must be equal to 'root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard'
            • for 'select' the following must be true:
              • property must be equal to 'requiredsecurityproperties'
            State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25336901 (cmdlet_state)
            State Comment:requiredsecurityproperties must match a pattern of 2
            State Requirements:
            • for all 'value' the following must be true:
              • all requiredsecurityproperties must match the pattern '2'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • module_name equals 'CimCmdlets'
            • module_id equals '{fb6cc51d-c096-4b38-b78d-0fed6277096a}'
            • module_version equals '1.0.0.0'
            • verb equals 'Get'
            • noun equals 'CimInstance'
            • collected 'parameters' result:
              • classname = 'Win32_DeviceGuard'
              • namespace = 'root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard'
            • collected 'select' result:
              • property = 'requiredsecurityproperties'
            • collected 'value' result:
              • requiredsecurityproperties = '{0}'
            Result Analysis:requiredsecurityproperties = '{0}' failed test of 'pattern match' compared to '2'
            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25336902 (cmdlet_test)
            Result:false
            Title:VirtualizationBasedSecurityStatus should return a value of 2
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25336902 (cmdlet_object)
            Object Comment:DeviceGuard virtualizationbasedsecuritystatus
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'module_name' must be equal to 'CimCmdlets'
            • check = 'verb' must be equal to 'Get'
            • check = 'noun' must be equal to 'CimInstance'
            • for 'parameters' the following must be true:
              • classname must be equal to 'Win32_DeviceGuard'
              • namespace must be equal to 'root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard'

            V-253371 - Virtualization-based protection of code integrity must be enabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253371r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000080
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Virtualization-based protection of code integrity enforces kernel mode memory protections as well as protecting Code Integrity validation paths. This isolates the processes from the rest of the operating system and can only be accessed by privileged system software.
            Check Text:Confirm virtualization-based protection of code integrity.

            For those devices that support the virtualization-based security (VBS) feature for protection of code integrity, this must be enabled. If the system meets the hardware, firmware and compatible device driver dependencies for enabling virtualization-based protection of code integrity but it is not enabled, this is a CAT II finding.

            Virtualization-based security currently cannot be implemented in virtual desktop implementations (VDI) due to specific supporting requirements including a TPM, UEFI with Secure Boot, and the capability to run the Hyper-V feature within the virtual desktop.

            For VDIs where the virtual desktop instance is deleted or refreshed upon logoff, this is NA.

            Run "PowerShell" with elevated privileges (run as administrator).
            Enter the following:
            "Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_DeviceGuard -Namespace root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard"

            If "SecurityServicesRunning" does not include a value of "2" (e.g., "{1, 2}"), this is a finding.

            Alternately:

            Run "System Information".
            Under "System Summary", verify the following:
            If "Virtualization-based Security Services Running" does not list "Hypervisor enforced Code Integrity", this is finding.

            The policy settings referenced in the Fix section will configure the following registry value. However due to hardware requirements, the registry value alone does not ensure proper function.

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard\

            Value Name: HypervisorEnforcedCodeIntegrity
            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000001 (1) (Enabled with UEFI lock), or 0x00000002 (2) (Enabled without lock)
            Fix Text:Virtualization-based security currently cannot be implemented in virtual desktop implementations (VDI) due to specific supporting requirements including a TPM, UEFI with Secure Boot, and the capability to run the Hyper-V feature within the virtual desktop.

            For VDIs where the virtual desktop instance is deleted or refreshed upon logoff, this is NA.

            Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Device Guard >> "Turn On virtualization-based Security" to "Enabled" with "Enabled with UEFI lock" or "Enabled without lock" selected for "virtualization-based Protection of Code Integrity:".

            "Enabled with UEFI lock" is preferred as more secure, however it cannot be turned off remotely through a group policy change if there is an issue.

            "Enabled without lock" will allow this to be turned off remotely while testing for issues.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253371
            Definition Result:false
            Title:Virtualization-based protection of code integrity must be enabled.
            Description:Credential Guard uses virtualization-based security to protect data that could be used in credential theft attacks if compromised. This authentication information, which was stored in the Local Security Authority (LSA) in previous versions of Windows, is isolated from the rest of operating system and can only be accessed by privileged system software.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Verify SecurityServicesRunning includes a value of 2)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25337101 (cmdlet_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Verify SecurityServicesRunning includes a value of 2
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25337101 (cmdlet_object)
            Object Comment:DeviceGuard SecurityServicesRunning
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'module_name' must be equal to 'CimCmdlets'
            • check = 'verb' must be equal to 'Get'
            • check = 'noun' must be equal to 'CimInstance'
            • for 'parameters' the following must be true:
              • classname must be equal to 'Win32_DeviceGuard'
              • namespace must be equal to 'root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard'
            • for 'select' the following must be true:
              • property must be equal to 'securityservicesrunning'
            State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25337101 (cmdlet_state)
            State Comment:SecurityServicesRunning equal to 2 as formatted array element
            State Requirements:
            • for all 'value' the following must be true:
              • all securityservicesrunning must match the pattern '2'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • module_name equals 'CimCmdlets'
            • module_id equals '{fb6cc51d-c096-4b38-b78d-0fed6277096a}'
            • module_version equals '1.0.0.0'
            • verb equals 'Get'
            • noun equals 'CimInstance'
            • collected 'parameters' result:
              • classname = 'Win32_DeviceGuard'
              • namespace = 'root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard'
            • collected 'select' result:
              • property = 'securityservicesrunning'
            • collected 'value' result:
              • securityservicesrunning = '{0}'
            Result Analysis:securityservicesrunning = '{0}' failed test of 'pattern match' compared to '2'

            V-253372 - Early Launch Antimalware, Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy must prevent boot drivers.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253372r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000085
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: The default behavior is for Early Launch Antimalware - Boot-Start Driver Initialization policy is to enforce "Good, unknown and bad but critical" (preventing "bad"). By being launched first by the kernel, ELAM ( Early Launch Antimalware) is ensured to be launched before any third-party software, and is therefore able to detect malware in the boot process and prevent it from initializing.
            Check Text:The default behavior is for Early Launch Antimalware - Boot-Start Driver Initialization policy is to enforce "Good, unknown and bad but critical" (preventing "bad").

            If the registry value name below does not exist, this is a finding.

            If it exists and is configured with a value of "7", this is a finding.

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Policies\EarlyLaunch\

            Value Name: DriverLoadPolicy

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1, 3, or 8

            Possible values for this setting are:
            8 - Good only
            1 - Good and unknown
            3 - Good, unknown and bad but critical
            7 - All (which includes "Bad" and would be a finding)
            Fix Text:Ensure that Early Launch Antimalware - Boot-Start Driver Initialization policy is set to enforce "Good, unknown and bad but critical" (preventing "bad").

            To correct this, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Early Launch Antimalware >> "Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy" to "Enabled with "Good, unknown and bad but critical" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253372
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000085 - Early Launch Antimalware, Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy must prevent boot drivers.
            Description:The default behavior is for Early Launch Antimalware - Boot-Start Driver Initialization policy is to enforce "Good, unknown and bad but critical" (preventing "bad"). By being launched first by the kernel, ELAM ( Early Launch Antimalware) is ensured to be launched before any third-party software, and is therefore able to detect malware in the boot process and prevent it from initializing.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy' is not set to 'Enabled' with 'All' selected)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337200 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy' is not set to 'Enabled' with 'All' selected
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337200 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\EarlyLaunch'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DriverLoadPolicy'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000003 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '3'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25337200 (registry_state)
            State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 8
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '8'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\EarlyLaunch\DriverLoadPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253373 - Group Policy objects must be reprocessed even if they have not changed.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253373r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000090
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Enabling this setting and then selecting the "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed" option ensures that the policies will be reprocessed even if none have been changed. This way, any unauthorized changes are forced to match the domain-based group policy settings again.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Group Policy\{35378EAC-683F-11D2-A89A-00C04FBBCFA2}

            Value Name: NoGPOListChanges

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Group Policy >> "Configure registry policy processing" to "Enabled" and select the option "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253373
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000090 - Group Policy objects must be reprocessed even if they have not changed.
            Description:Enabling this setting and then selecting the "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed" option ensures that the policies will be reprocessed even if none have been changed. This way, any unauthorized changes are forced to match the domain-based group policy settings again.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (NoGPOistChanges is set to 0.)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337300 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:NoGPOistChanges is set to 0.
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337300 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Group Policy\{35378EAC-683F-11D2-A89A-00C04FBBCFA2}'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoGPOListChanges'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Group Policy\{35378EAC-683F-11D2-A89A-00C04FBBCFA2}\NoGPOListChanges'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253374 - Downloading print driver packages over HTTP must be prevented.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253374r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000100
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting prevents the computer from downloading print driver packages over HTTP.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers\

            Value Name: DisableWebPnPDownload

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Internet Communication Management >> Internet Communication settings >> "Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253374
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000100 - Downloading print driver packages over HTTP must be prevented.
            Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting prevents the computer from downloading print driver packages over HTTP.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337400 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337400 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableWebPnPDownload'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers\DisableWebPnPDownload'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253375r958478_rule

            Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting prevents Windows from downloading a list of providers for the Web publishing and online ordering wizards.

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE

This setting prevents Windows from downloading a list of providers for the Web publishing and online ordering wizards.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Turn off Internet download for Web publishing and online ordering wizards' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337500 (registry_test)

            V-253376 - Printing over HTTP must be prevented.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253376r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000110
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting prevents the client computer from printing over HTTP, which allows the computer to print to printers on the intranet as well as the internet.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers\

            Value Name: DisableHTTPPrinting

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Internet Communication Management >> Internet Communication settings >> "Turn off printing over HTTP" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253376
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000110 - Printing over HTTP must be prevented.
            Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting prevents the client computer from printing over HTTP, which allows the computer to print to printers on the intranet as well as the internet.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Turn off printing over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337600 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Turn off printing over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337600 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableHTTPPrinting'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers\DisableHTTPPrinting'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253389 - Enhanced anti-spoofing for facial recognition must be enabled on Windows 11.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253389r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000195
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Enhanced anti-spoofing provides additional protections when using facial recognition with devices that support it.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Biometrics\FacialFeatures\

            Value Name: EnhancedAntiSpoofing

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000001 (1)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Biometrics >> Facial Features >> "Configure enhanced anti-spoofing" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:

            Title:WN11-CC-000195 - Enhanced anti-spoofing for facial recognition must be enabled on Windows 11.
            Description:Enhanced anti-spoofing provides additional protections when using facial recognition with devices that support it.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Configure enhanced anti-spoofing' is set to 'Enabled')

            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338900 (registry_test)
            Result:false

            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

            Object Comment:

            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Biometrics\FacialFeatures'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnhancedAntiSpoofing'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment:
