            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Biometrics\FacialFeatures\EnhancedAntiSpoofing'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253391 - Administrator accounts must not be enumerated during elevation.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253391r958518_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000200
            Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
            Description: Enumeration of administrator accounts when elevating can provide part of the logon information to an unauthorized user. This setting configures the system to always require users to type in a username and password to elevate a running application.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\CredUI\

            Value Name: EnumerateAdministrators

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Credential User Interface >> "Enumerate administrator accounts on elevation" to "Disabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253391
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000200 - Administrator accounts must not be enumerated during elevation.
            Description:Enumeration of administrator accounts when elevating can provide part of the logon information to an unauthorized user. This setting configures the system to always require users to type in a username and password to elevate a running application.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Enumerate administrator accounts on elevation' is set to 'Disabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339100 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Enumerate administrator accounts on elevation' is set to 'Disabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339100 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\CredUI'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnumerateAdministrators'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\CredUI\EnumerateAdministrators'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253392 - Enhanced diagnostic data must be limited to the minimum required to support Windows Analytics.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253392r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000204
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Limiting this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise. The "Enhanced" level for telemetry includes additional information beyond "Security" and "Basic" on how Windows and apps are used and advanced reliability data. Windows Analytics can use a "limited enhanced" level to provide information such as health data for devices.
            Check Text:If "Enhanced" level is enabled for telemetry, this must be configured. If "Security" or "Basic" are configured, this is NA. (See WN11-CC-000205).

            If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\

            Value Name: LimitEnhancedDiagnosticDataWindowsAnalytics

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000001 (1)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Data Collection and Preview Builds >> "Limit optional diagnostic data for Windows Analytics" to "Enabled" with "Enable Desktop Analytics collection" selected in "Options:".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253392
            Definition Result:false
            Title:Enhanced diagnostic data must be limited to the minimum required to support Windows Analytics.
            Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Limiting this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise. The "Enhanced" level for telemetry includes additional information beyond "Security" and "Basic" on how Windows and apps are used and advanced reliability data. Windows Analytics can use a "limited enhanced" level to provide information such as health data for devices.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (Enhanced diagnostic data must be limited to the minimum required to support Windows Analytics.)
              • false (Enhanced diagnostic data must be limited to the minimum required to support Windows Analytics.)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25339201 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Enhanced diagnostic data must be limited to the minimum required to support Windows Analytics.
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25339201 (registry_object)
            Object Comment:DataCollection LimitEnhancedDiagnosticDataWindowsAnalytics Setting
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'LimitEnhancedDiagnosticDataWindowsAnalytics'
            State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25339201 (registry_state)
            State Comment:Value Equal To 1
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\LimitEnhancedDiagnosticDataWindowsAnalytics'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25339202 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Enhanced diagnostic data must be limited to the minimum required to support Windows Analytics.
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25339202 (registry_object)
            Object Comment:DataCollection AllowTelemetry Setting
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowTelemetry'
            State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25339202 (registry_state)
            State Comment:Value Equal To 1
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be less than or equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\AllowTelemetry'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253393 - Windows Telemetry must not be configured to Full.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253393r958564_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000205
            Identities:CCI-001312 (NIST SP 800-53: SI-11 b; NIST SP 800-53A: SI-11.1 (iii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SI-11 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SI-11 a)
            Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Limiting this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise. The "Security" option for Telemetry configures the lowest amount of data, effectively none outside of the Malicious Software Removal Tool (MSRT), Defender and telemetry client settings. "Basic" sends basic diagnostic and usage data and may be required to support some Microsoft services. "Enhanced" includes additional information on how Windows and apps are used and advanced reliability data. Windows Analytics can use a "limited enhanced" level to provide information such as health data for devices.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\

            Value Name: AllowTelemetry

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000000 (0) (Security)
            0x00000001 (1) (Basic)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Data Collection and Preview Builds >> "Allow Diagnostic Data" to "Enabled" with "Send required diagnostic data" selected in "Options:".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253393
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000205 - Windows Telemetry must not be configured to Full.
            Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Limiting this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise. The "Security" option for Telemetry configures the lowest amount of data, effectively none outside of the Malicious Software Removal Tool (MSRT), Defender and telemetry client settings. "Basic" sends basic diagnostic and usage data and may be required to support some Microsoft services. "Enhanced" includes additional information on how Windows and apps are used and advanced reliability data. Windows Analytics can use a "limited enhanced" level to provide information such as health data for devices.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Allow Diagnostic Data' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Diagnostic Data Off (Not recommended)' or 'Send Required Diagnostic Data' selected in 'Options:')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339300 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Allow Diagnostic Data' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Diagnostic Data Off (Not recommended)' or 'Send Required Diagnostic Data' selected in 'Options:'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339300 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowTelemetry'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\AllowTelemetry'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253395 - The Microsoft Defender SmartScreen for Explorer must be enabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253395r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000210
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: Microsoft Defender SmartScreen helps protect systems from programs downloaded from the internet that may be malicious. Enabling Microsoft Defender SmartScreen will warn or prevent users from running potentially malicious programs.
            Check Text:This is applicable to unclassified systems, for other systems this is NA.

            If the following registry values do not exist or are not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\

            Value Name: EnableSmartScreen

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000001 (1)

            And

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\

            Value Name: ShellSmartScreenLevel

            Value Type: REG_SZ
            Value: Block
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> File Explorer >> "Configure Windows Defender SmartScreen" to "Enabled" with "Warn and prevent bypass" selected.

            Windows 11 includes duplicate policies for this setting. It can also be configured under Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Defender SmartScreen >> Explorer.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253395
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000210 - The Microsoft Defender SmartScreen for Explorer must be enabled.
            Description:Microsoft Defender SmartScreen helps protect systems from programs downloaded from the internet that may be malicious. Enabling Microsoft Defender SmartScreen will warn or prevent users from running potentially malicious programs.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Configure Windows SmartScreen' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339500 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Configure Windows SmartScreen' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339500 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnableSmartScreen'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\EnableSmartScreen'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253399 - Windows 11 must be configured to disable Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253399r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Applicability:Windows_NotEnterpriseLTSC
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000252
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting is intended for use with games; however, it could potentially record screen shots of other applications and expose sensitive data. Disabling the feature will prevent this from occurring.
            Check Text:This is NA for Windows 11 LTSC.

            If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\GameDVR\

            Value Name: AllowGameDVR

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000000 (0)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting >> "Enables or disables Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting" to "Disabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:

            Description:Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting is intended for use with games; however, it could potentially record screen shots of other applications and expose sensitive data. Disabling the feature will prevent this from occurring.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Enables or disables Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting' is set to 'Disabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339900 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Enables or disables Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting' is set to 'Disabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339900 (registry_object)
            Object Comment:

            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\GameDVR'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowGameDVR'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment:

            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\GameDVR\AllowGameDVR'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253401 - Windows 11 must be configured to require a minimum pin length of six characters or greater.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253401r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000260
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Windows allows the use of PINs as well as biometrics for authentication without sending a password to a network or website where it could be compromised. Longer minimum PIN lengths increase the available combinations an attacker would have to attempt. Shorter minimum length significantly reduces the strength.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\PassportForWork\PINComplexity\

            Value Name: MinimumPINLength

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 6 (or greater)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> PIN Complexity >> "Minimum PIN length" to "6" or greater.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253401
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000260 - Windows 11 must be configured to require a minimum pin length of six characters or greater.
            Description:Windows allows the use of PINs as well as biometrics for authentication without sending a password to a network or website where it could be compromised. Longer minimum PIN lengths increase the available combinations an attacker would have to attempt. Shorter minimum length significantly reduces the strength.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Minimum PIN length' is 6 or greater.)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340100 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Minimum PIN length' is 6 or greater.
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340100 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\PassportForWork\PINComplexity'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'MinimumPINLength'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25340100 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '6'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\PassportForWork\PINComplexity\MinimumPINLength'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253402 - Passwords must not be saved in the Remote Desktop Client.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253402r1051051_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000270
            Identities:CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-11)
            Description: Saving passwords in the Remote Desktop Client could allow an unauthorized user to establish a remote desktop session to another system. The system must be configured to prevent users from saving passwords in the Remote Desktop Client.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\

            Value Name: DisablePasswordSaving

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Connection Client >> "Do not allow passwords to be saved" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253402
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000270 - Passwords must not be saved in the Remote Desktop Client.
            Description:Saving passwords in the Remote Desktop Client could allow an unauthorized user to establish a remote desktop session to another system. The system must be configured to prevent users from saving passwords in the Remote Desktop Client.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Do not allow passwords to be saved' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340200 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Do not allow passwords to be saved' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340200 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisablePasswordSaving'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253403r1137695_rule

            V-253404 - Remote Desktop Services must always prompt a client for passwords upon connection.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253404r1051052_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000280
            Identities:CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-11)
            Description: This setting controls the ability of users to supply passwords automatically as part of their remote desktop connection. Disabling this setting would allow anyone to use the stored credentials in a connection item to connect to the terminal server.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\

            Value Name: fPromptForPassword

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Session Host >> Security >> "Always prompt for password upon connection" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0

            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA

            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:

            Definition Result:false

            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))

            Result:false

            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340400 (registry_object)

            V-253405 - The Remote Desktop Session Host must require secure RPC communications.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253405r991554_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000285
            Identities:CCI-001453 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (2))
            Description: Allowing unsecure RPC communication exposes the system to man in the middle attacks and data disclosure attacks. A man in the middle attack occurs when an intruder captures packets between a client and server and modifies them before allowing the packets to be exchanged. Usually the attacker will modify the information in the packets in an attempt to cause either the client or server to reveal sensitive information.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\

            Value Name: fEncryptRPCTraffic

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Session Host >> Security "Require secure RPC communication" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253405
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000285 - The Remote Desktop Session Host must require secure RPC communications.
            Description:Allowing unsecure RPC communication exposes the system to man in the middle attacks and data disclosure attacks. A man in the middle attack occurs when an intruder captures packets between a client and server and modifies them before allowing the packets to be exchanged. Usually the attacker will modify the information in the packets in an attempt to cause either the client or server to reveal sensitive information.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Require secure RPC communication' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340500 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Require secure RPC communication' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340500 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'fEncryptRPCTraffic'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\fEncryptRPCTraffic'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253406 - Remote Desktop Services must be configured with the client connection encryption set to the required level.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253406r958408_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000290
            Identities:CCI-000068 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (2))
            Description: Remote connections must be encrypted to prevent interception of data or sensitive information. Selecting "High Level" will ensure encryption of Remote Desktop Services sessions in both directions.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 3

            Weight:10.0

            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11

            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11

            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))

                • false ('Set client connection encryption level' is set to 'Enabled' and 'High Level')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340600 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Set client connection encryption level' is set to 'Enabled' and 'High Level'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340600 (registry_object)

            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'MinEncryptionLevel'

            V-253413 - Automatically signing in the last interactive user after a system-initiated restart must be disabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253413r991591_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000325
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Windows can be configured to automatically sign the user back in after a Windows Update restart. Some protections are in place to help ensure this is done in a secure fashion; however, disabling this will prevent the caching of credentials for this purpose and also ensure the user is aware of the restart.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

            Value Name: DisableAutomaticRestartSignOn

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Logon Options >> "Sign-in last interactive user automatically after a system-initiated restart" to "Disabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253413
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000325 - Automatically signing in the last interactive user after a system-initiated restart must be disabled.
            Description:Windows can be configured to automatically sign the user back in after a Windows Update restart. Some protections are in place to help ensure this is done in a secure fashion; however, disabling this will prevent the caching of credentials for this purpose and also ensure the user is aware of the restart.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (Verifies 'Sign-in last interactive user automatically after a system-initiated restart' is set to 'Disabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341300 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Verifies 'Sign-in last interactive user automatically after a system-initiated restart' is set to 'Disabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341300 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableAutomaticRestartSignOn'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\DisableAutomaticRestartSignOn'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253414 - PowerShell script block logging must be enabled on Windows 11.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253414r958422_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000326
            Identities:CCI-000135 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 (1))
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Enabling PowerShell script block logging will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\ScriptBlockLogging\

            Value Name: EnableScriptBlockLogging

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows PowerShell >> "Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253414
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000326 - PowerShell script block logging must be enabled on Windows 11.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Enabling PowerShell script block logging will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341400 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341400 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\ScriptBlockLogging'
            • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'EnableScriptBlockLogging'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\ScriptBlockLogging\EnableScriptBlockLogging'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253415 - PowerShell Transcription must be enabled on Windows 11.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253415r958420_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000327
            Identities:CCI-000134 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 e)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Enabling PowerShell Transcription will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\Transcription\

            Value Name: EnableTranscripting

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows PowerShell >> "Turn on PowerShell Transcription" to "Enabled".

            Specify the Transcript output directory to point to a Central Log Server or another secure location to prevent user access.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253415
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000327 - PowerShell Transcription must be enabled on Windows 11.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

            Enabling PowerShell Transcription will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (PowerShell Transcription is enabled on Windows 11)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341500 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:PowerShell Transcription is enabled on Windows 11
            Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341500 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\Transcription'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnableTranscripting'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\Transcription\EnableTranscripting'. Existence requirement is 'all_exist' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253417 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not allow unencrypted traffic.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253417r958848_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000335
            Identities:CCI-002890 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: MA-4 (6); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: MA-4 (6))
            Description: Unencrypted remote access to a system can allow sensitive information to be compromised. Windows remote management connections must be encrypted to prevent this.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Client\

            Value Name: AllowUnencryptedTraffic

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Remote Management (WinRM) >> WinRM Client >> "Allow unencrypted traffic" to "Disabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253417
            Definition Result:false

            Description:Unencrypted remote access to a system can allow sensitive information to be compromised. Windows remote management connections must be encrypted to prevent this.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:

              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Allow unencrypted traffic' is set to 'Disabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341700 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Allow unencrypted traffic' is set to 'Disabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341700 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Client'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowUnencryptedTraffic'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Client\AllowUnencryptedTraffic'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253420 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must not store RunAs credentials.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253420r1051055_rule

            Version:WN11-CC-000355
            Identities:CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-11)

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Service\

            Value Name: DisableRunAs

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Remote Management (WinRM) >> WinRM Service >> "Disallow WinRM from storing RunAs credentials" to "Enabled".

            V-253422 - Windows 11 must be configured to prevent Windows apps from being activated by voice while the system is locked.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253422r958400_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000365
            Identities:CCI-000056 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-11 b; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-11.1 (iii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-11 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-11 b)
            Description: Allowing Windows apps to be activated by voice from the lock screen could allow for unauthorized use. Requiring logon will ensure the apps are only used by authorized personnel.
            Check Text:The setting is NA when the "Allow voice activation" policy is configured to disallow applications to be activated with voice for all users.
            If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppPrivacy\

            Value Name: LetAppsActivateWithVoiceAboveLock

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000002 (2)

            If the following registry value exists and is configured as specified, requirement is NA:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppPrivacy\

            Value Name: LetAppsActivateWithVoice

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000002 (2)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> App Privacy >> "Let Windows apps activate with voice while the system is locked" to "Enabled" with Default for all Apps: set to Force Deny.

            The requirement is NA if the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> App Privacy >> "Let Windows apps activate with voice" is configured to "Enabled" with Default for all Apps: set to Force Deny.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253422
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000365 - Windows 11 must be configured to prevent Windows apps from being activated by voice while the system is locked.
            Description:Allowing Windows apps to be activated by voice from the lock screen could allow for unauthorized use. Requiring logon will ensure the apps are only used by authorized personnel.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Let Windows apps activate with voice' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Default for all Apps' set to 'Force Deny')
                • false ('Let Windows apps activate with voice while the system is locked' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Default for all Apps' set to 'Force Deny')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342200 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Let Windows apps activate with voice' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Default for all Apps' set to 'Force Deny'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342200 (registry_object)
            Object Comment:

            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppPrivacy'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'LetAppsActivateWithVoice'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)

            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342201 (registry_test)
            Result:false

            V-253423 - The convenience PIN for Windows 11 must be disabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253423r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000370
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: This policy controls whether a domain user can sign in using a convenience PIN to prevent enabling (Password Stuffer).
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System

            Value Name: AllowDomainPINLogon
            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value data: 0
            Fix Text:Disable the convenience PIN sign-in.

            To correct this, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Logon >> Set "Turn on convenience PIN sign-in" to "Disabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253423
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000370 - The convenience PIN for Windows 11 must be disabled.
            Description:This policy controls whether a domain user can sign in using a convenience PIN to prevent enabling (Password Stuffer).
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Turn on convenience PIN sign-in' is 'Disabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342300 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Turn on convenience PIN sign-in' is 'Disabled'
            Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342300 (registry_object)
            Object Comment:
