            V-253466 - The system must be configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253466r1137699_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-SO-000230
            Identities:CCI-002450 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-13; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-13 b)
            Description: This setting ensures that the system uses algorithms that are FIPS-compliant for encryption, hashing, and signing. FIPS-compliant algorithms meet specific standards established by the U.S. Government and must be the algorithms used for all OS encryption functions.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy\

            Value Name: Enabled

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1

            Warning: Clients with this setting enabled will not be able to communicate via digitally encrypted or signed protocols with servers that do not support these algorithms.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253466
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-SO-000230 - The system must be configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing.
            Description:This setting ensures that the system uses algorithms that are FIPS-compliant for encryption, hashing, and signing. FIPS-compliant algorithms meet specific standards established by the U.S. Government and must be the algorithms used for all OS encryption functions.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346600 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346600 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'Enabled'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy'
            • name equals 'Enabled'
            • last_write_time equals '132963747150000000'
            • type equals 'reg_dword'
            • value equals '0'
            • windows_view equals '64_bit'
            Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy\Enabled\value = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

            V-253468 - User Account Control approval mode for the built-in Administrator must be enabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253468r1051057_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-SO-000245
            Identities:CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-11)
            Description: User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures the built-in Administrator account so that it runs in Admin Approval Mode.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

            Value Name: FilterAdministratorToken

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account" to "Enabled".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:

            Definition Result:false

            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (Verifies 'User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346800 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Verifies 'User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account' is set to 'Enabled'

            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346800 (registry_object)

            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'FilterAdministratorToken'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment:

            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'

            V-253469 - User Account Control must prompt administrators for consent on the secure desktop.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253469r958518_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-SO-000250
            Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
            Description: User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures the elevation requirements for logged on administrators to complete a task that requires raised privileges.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

            Value Name: ConsentPromptBehaviorAdmin

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 2 (Prompt for consent on the secure desktop)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators in Admin Approval Mode" to "Prompt for consent on the secure desktop".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253469
            Definition Result:false

This setting configures the elevation requirements for logged on administrators to complete a task that requires raised privileges.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:

              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators in Admin Approval Mode' is set to 'Prompt for consent on the secure desktop')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346900 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators in Admin Approval Mode' is set to 'Prompt for consent on the secure desktop'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.

            Collected Item/State Result:

            • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'

            V-253471 - User Account Control must automatically deny elevation requests for standard users.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253471r1051058_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-SO-000255
            Identities:CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-11)
            Description: User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. Denying elevation requests from standard user accounts requires tasks that need elevation to be initiated by accounts with administrative privileges. This ensures correct accounts are used on the system for privileged tasks to help mitigate credential theft.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

            Value Name: ConsentPromptBehaviorUser

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users" to "Automatically deny elevation requests".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253471
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-SO-000255 - User Account Control must automatically deny elevation requests for standard users.
            Description:User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. Denying elevation requests from standard user accounts requires tasks that need elevation to be initiated by accounts with administrative privileges. This ensures correct accounts are used on the system for privileged tasks to help mitigate credential theft.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (Verifies 'User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users' is set to 'Automatically deny elevation requests')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25347100 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Verifies 'User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users' is set to 'Automatically deny elevation requests'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25347100 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'ConsentPromptBehaviorUser'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment:

            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • key equals 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'

            Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\ConsentPromptBehaviorUser\value = '3' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

            V-253476 - Passwords for enabled local Administrator accounts must be changed at least every 60 days.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253476r1051060_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Applicability:local_enabled_administrators
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-SO-000280
            Identities:CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
            CCI-000199 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (d); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (d))
            Description: The longer a password is in use, the greater the opportunity for someone to gain unauthorized knowledge of the password. A local Administrator account is not generally used and its password may not be changed as frequently as necessary. Changing the password for enabled Administrator accounts on a regular basis will limit its exposure.

            Windows LAPS must be used to change the built-in Administrator account password.
            Check Text:If there are no enabled local Administrator accounts, this is Not Applicable.

            Review the password last set date for the enabled local Administrator account.

            On the standalone or domain-joined workstation:

            Open "PowerShell".

            Enter "Get-LocalUser -Name * | Select-Object *".

            If the "PasswordLastSet" date is greater than "60" days old for the local Administrator account for administering the computer/domain, this is a finding.

            Verify LAPS is configured and operational.

            Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> LAPS >> Password Settings >> Set to enabled. Password Complexity, large letters + small letters + numbers + special, Password Length 14, Password Age 60. If not configured as shown, this is a finding.

            Verify LAPS Operational logs >> Event Viewer >> Applications and Services Logs >> Microsoft >> Windows >> LAPS >> Operational. Verify LAPS policy process is completing. If it is not, this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Change the enabled local Administrator account password at least every 60 days.

            Windows LAPS must be used to change the built-in Administrator account password. Domain-joined and nondomain-joined systems can configure this to occur more frequently. LAPS will change the password every 30 days by default.

            More information is available at:
            https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/windows-it-pro-blog/by-popular-demand-windows-laps-available-now/ba-p/3788747
            https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/laps/laps-overview#windows-laps-supported-platforms-and-azure-ad-laps-preview-status
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253476
            Definition Result:false
            Title:Passwords for enabled local Administrator accounts must be changed at least every 60 days.
            Description:Windows Server passwords for the built-in Administrator account must be changed at least every 60 days.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Password Age Check: Verify all enabled local Administrator accounts passwords are less than 60 days old)
              • false (LAPS Compliance: Password Age - 60)
              • false (LAPS Compliance: Password Length - 14)
              • false (LAPS Compliance: Password Complexity - large letters + small letters + numbers + specials)
              • false (Verify LAPS policy process is completing)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25347601 (user_test)
            Result:true
            Title:Password Age Check: Verify all enabled local Administrator accounts passwords are less than 60 days old
            Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25347601 (user_object)
            Object Comment:Enabled Local Administrators
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'user' must match the pattern '.*'
            Include Items If:
            • group equals 'Administrators'
            Include Items If:
            • enabled equals 'true'
            State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25347601 (user_state)
            State Comment:Confirm that all enabled local Administrators have a password age less than or equal to 60 days
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'password_age_days' must be less than or equal to '60'
            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25347602 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:LAPS Compliance: Password Age - 60
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25347602 (registry_object)
            Object Comment:Windows LAPS PasswordAgeDays
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'PasswordAgeDays'
            State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25347602 (registry_state)
            State Comment:Registry value must be of type reg_dword and value less than or equals 60
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be less than or equal to '60'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS\PasswordAgeDays'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25347603 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:LAPS Compliance: Password Length - 14
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25347603 (registry_object)
            Object Comment:Windows LAPS PasswordLength
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'PasswordLength'
            State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25347603 (registry_state)

            State Requirements:

            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '14'

            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25347604 (registry_test)

            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'PasswordComplexity'
            State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25347604 (registry_state)
            State Comment:Registry value must be of type reg_dword and value equals 4
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '4'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS\PasswordComplexity'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25347605 (shellcommand_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Verify LAPS policy process is completing
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25347605 (shellcommand_object)
            Object Comment:Operational logs - Event Viewer - Applications and Services Logs - Microsoft - Windows - LAPS - Operational
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'shell' must be equal to 'powershell'
            • check = 'command' must be equal to 'get-winevent -logname Microsoft-Windows-LAPS/Operational -MaxEvents 50'
            • check = 'pattern' must be equal to 'LAPS policy processing (\w+)\.'
            State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25347605 (shellcommand_state)
            State Comment:LAPS Operational
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'shell' must be equal to 'powershell'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'command' must be equal to 'get-winevent -logname Microsoft-Windows-LAPS/Operational -MaxEvents 50'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'subexpression' must match the pattern 'succeeded'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • shell equals 'powershell'
            • command equals 'get-winevent -logname Microsoft-Windows-LAPS/Operational -MaxEvents 50'
            • pattern equals 'LAPS policy processing (\w+)\.'
            • exit_status equals ''
            • stderr_line equals 'get-winevent : No events were found that match the specified selection criteria.'
            • stderr_line equals 'At line:1 char:1'
            • stderr_line equals '+ get-winevent -logname Microsoft-Windows-LAPS/Operational -MaxEvents 5 ...'
            • stderr_line equals '+ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~'
            • stderr_line equals ' + CategoryInfo : ObjectNotFound: (:) [Get-WinEvent], Exception'
            • stderr_line equals ' + FullyQualifiedErrorId : NoMatchingEventsFound,Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.GetWinEventCommand'
            • stderr_line equals ' '
            Result Analysis:Failed state entity existence check. , Element: 'subexpression' does not exist.

            V-253480 - The "Access this computer from the network" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Remote Desktop Users groups.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253480r1137691_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-UR-000010
            Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
            Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            Accounts with the "Access this computer from the network" user right may access resources on the system, and must be limited to those that require it.
            Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
            Run "gpedit.msc".

            Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

            If any groups or accounts other than the following are granted the "Access this computer from the network" user right, this is a finding:

            Administrators
            Remote Desktop Users

            If a domain application account such as for a management tool requires this user right, this would not be a finding.

            Vendor documentation must support the requirement for having the user right.

            The requirement must be documented with the ISSO.

            The application account, managed at the domain level, must meet requirements for application account passwords, such as length and frequency of changes as defined in the Windows Server STIGs.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Access this computer from the network" to only include the following groups or accounts:

            Administrators
            Remote Desktop Users
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253480
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-UR-000010 - The "Access this computer from the network" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Remote Desktop Users groups.
            Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            Accounts with the "Access this computer from the network" user right may access resources on the system, and must be limited to those that require it.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (The 'Access this computer from the network' user right is only assigned to the Administrators and Remote Desktop Users groups.)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348000 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:The 'Access this computer from the network' user right is only assigned to the Administrators and Remote Desktop Users groups.
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000010 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment:Administrators, Remote Desktop Users
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            Exclude Items If:
            • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
            Exclude Items If:
            • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Remote Desktop Users'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348000 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'senetworklogonright' must be equal to '0'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • senetworklogonright equals '1' for: Everyone, Backup Operators, Users
            • senetworklogonright equals '0' for Event Log Readers, NETWORK SERVICE, INTERACTIVE, SERVICE, Performance Log Users, Network Configuration Operators, LocalGuest, Authenticated Users, LOCAL SERVICE, System Managed Accounts Group, NETWORK, Guests, CREATOR OWNER, Replicator, TERMINAL SERVER USER, WDAGUtilityAccount, BATCH, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DESKTOP-CRJIC3S\Derrick lattimore, Access Control Assistance Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, IIS_IUSRS, Distributed COM Users, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, PROXY, Power Users, Device Owners, CREATOR GROUP, SYSTEM, LocalAdmin, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Hyper-V Administrators, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, IUSR, DefaultAccount, Remote Management Users, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, Performance Monitor Users
            Result Analysis:senetworklogonright : Everyone = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
            senetworklogonright : Backup Operators = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
            senetworklogonright : Users = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

            V-253482 - The "Allow log on locally" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Users groups.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253482r1137691_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-UR-000025
            Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
            Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            Accounts with the "Allow log on locally" user right can log on interactively to a system.
            Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.

            Run "gpedit.msc".

            Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

            If any groups or accounts other than the following are granted the "Allow log on locally" user right, this is a finding:

            Administrators
            Users
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Allow log on locally" to only include the following groups or accounts:

            Administrators
            Users

            Weight:10.0

            V-253483 - The "Back up files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253483r958726_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-UR-000030
            Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
            Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            Accounts with the "Back up files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data."
            Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
            Run "gpedit.msc".

            Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

            If any groups or accounts other than the following are granted the "Back up files and directories" user right, this is a finding:

            Administrators
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Back up files and directories" to only include the following groups or accounts:

            Administrators

            V-253491 - The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253491r1137691_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-UR-000070
            Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
            Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            The "Deny access to this computer from the network" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

            In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

            Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

            The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
            Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.

            Run "gpedit.msc".

            Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

            If the following groups or accounts are not defined for the "Deny access to this computer from the network" right, this is a finding:

            Domain Systems Only:
            Enterprise Admins group
            Domain Admins group
            Local account (see Note below)

            All Systems:
            Guests group

            Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

            Note: "Local account" is a built-in security group used to assign user rights and permissions to all local accounts.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny access to this computer from the network" to include the following:

            Domain Systems Only:
            Enterprise Admins group
            Domain Admins group
            Local account (see Note below)

            All Systems:
            Guests group

            Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

            Note: "Local account" is a built-in security group used to assign user rights and permissions to all local accounts.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253491
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-UR-000070 - The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.
            Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            The "Deny access to this computer from the network" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

            In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

            Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

            The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                  • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                    • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Domain Admins)
                    • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Enterprise Admins)
                    • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account)
                    • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                      • false (The system is a Windows Domain Member Workstation)
                  • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                    • true (The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation)
                • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Guests)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349101 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Domain Admins
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenynetworklogonright' must be equal to '1'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
            • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
            Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349102 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Enterprise Admins
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenynetworklogonright' must be equal to '1'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
            • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
            Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349103 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000007 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenynetworklogonright' must be equal to '1'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
            • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
            Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:10000200 (wmi57_test)
            Result:false
            Title:The system is a Windows Domain Member Workstation
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
            • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000200 (wmi57_state)
            State Comment:Domain Member Workstation
            State Requirements:
            • for all 'result' the following must be true:
              • all domainrole must be equal to '1'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
            • wql equals 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
            • collected 'result' result:
              • domainrole = '0'
            Result Analysis:domainrole = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:10000100 (wmi57_test)
            Result:true
            Title:The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
            • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000100 (wmi57_state)
            State Comment:Standalone Workstation
            State Requirements:
            • for all 'result' the following must be true:
              • all domainrole must be equal to '0'
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349100 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Guests
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000006 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'security_principle' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guests'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenynetworklogonright' must be equal to '1'

            • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for Guests

            V-253494 - The "Deny log on locally" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253494r1137691_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-UR-000085
            Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
            Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            The "Deny log on locally" right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

            In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

            The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
            Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.

            Run "gpedit.msc".

            Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

            If the following groups or accounts are not defined for the "Deny log on locally" right, this is a finding.

            Domain Systems Only:
            Enterprise Admins Group
            Domain Admins Group

            Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

            All Systems:
            Guests Group
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on locally" to include the following:

            Domain Systems Only:
            Enterprise Admins Group
            Domain Admins Group

            Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

            All Systems:
            Guests Group
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253494
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-UR-000085 - The "Deny log on locally" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.
            Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            The "Deny log on locally" right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

            In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

            The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                  • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                    • false (Deny log on locally - Domain Admins)
                    • false (Deny log on as a locally - Enterprise Admins)
                  • true (System is not a member of a domain)
                • false (Deny log on locally - Guests)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349402 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Deny log on locally - Domain Admins
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349400 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment:Deny log on locally
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyinteractivelogonright' must be equal to '1'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
            • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for
            Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349403 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Deny log on as a locally - Enterprise Admins
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349400 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment:Deny log on locally
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyinteractivelogonright' must be equal to '1'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
            • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for
            Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349401 (wmi57_test)
            Result:true
            Title:System is not a member of a domain
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
            • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000100 (wmi57_state)
            State Comment:Standalone Workstation
            State Requirements:
            • for all 'result' the following must be true:
              • all domainrole must be equal to '0'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000300 (wmi57_state)
            State Comment:Standalone Server
            State Requirements:
            • for all 'result' the following must be true:
              • all domainrole must be equal to '2'
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349400 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Deny log on locally - Guests
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000006 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'security_principle' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guests'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349400 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment:Deny log on locally
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyinteractivelogonright' must be equal to '1'

            • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for Guests

            V-253495 - The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right on Windows 11 workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253495r1137691_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-UR-000090
            Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
            CCI-002314 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (1))
            Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on using Remote Desktop Services.

            If Remote Desktop Services is not used by the organization, the Everyone group must be assigned this right to prevent all access.

            In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

            Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

            The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
            Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.

            Run "gpedit.msc".

            Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

            If the following groups or accounts are not defined for the "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" right, this is a finding:

            If Remote Desktop Services is not used by the organization, the "Everyone" group can replace all of the groups listed below.

            Domain Systems Only:
            Enterprise Admins group
            Domain Admins group
            Local account (see Note below)

            All Systems:
            Guests group

            Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

            Note: "Local account" is a built-in security group used to assign user rights and permissions to all local accounts.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" to include the following:

            If Remote Desktop Services is not used by the organization, assign the Everyone group this right to prevent all access.

            Domain Systems Only:
            Enterprise Admins group
            Domain Admins group
            Local account (see Note below)

            All Systems:
            Guests group

            Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

            Note: "Local account" is a built-in security group used to assign user rights and permissions to all local accounts.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253495
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-UR-000090 - The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right on Windows 11 workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.
            Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on using Remote Desktop Services.

            If Remote Desktop Services is not used by the organization, the Everyone group must be assigned this right to prevent all access.

            In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

            Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

            The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                    • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                      • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Domain Admins)
                      • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Enterprise Admins)
                      • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Local account)
                      • false (System is a Windows non-DC domain-joined system)
                    • true (The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation or Server)
                  • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Guests)
                • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Everyone)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349501 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Domain Admins
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349500 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright' must be equal to '1'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
            • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '0' for
            Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349502 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Enterprise Admins
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349500 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright' must be equal to '1'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
            • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '0' for
            Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349503 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Local account
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000007 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349500 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright' must be equal to '1'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
            • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '0' for
            Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349504 (wmi57_test)
            Result:false
            Title:System is a Windows non-DC domain-joined system
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
            • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000200 (wmi57_state)
            State Comment:Domain Member Workstation
            State Requirements:
            • for all 'result' the following must be true:
              • all domainrole must be equal to '1'

            • for all 'result' the following must be true:
              • all domainrole must be equal to '3'

            V-253505 - The "Restore files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253505r958726_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-UR-000160
            Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
            Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            Accounts with the "Restore files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data. It could also be used to over-write more current data.
            Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
            Run "gpedit.msc".

            Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

            If any groups or accounts other than the following are granted the "Restore files and directories" user right, this is a finding:

            Administrators
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Restore files and directories" to only include the following groups or accounts:

            Administrators
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253505
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-UR-000160 - The "Restore files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
            Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

            Accounts with the "Restore files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data. It could also be used to over-write more current data.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (Restore files and directories - Administrators)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25350500 (accesstoken_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Restore files and directories - Administrators
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
            Object Comment:Administrators
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            Exclude Items If:
            • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25350500 (accesstoken_state)
            State Comment:Restore files and directories
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'serestoreprivilege' must be equal to '0'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • serestoreprivilege equals '1' for: Backup Operators
            • serestoreprivilege equals '0' for Event Log Readers, NETWORK SERVICE, INTERACTIVE, SERVICE, Performance Log Users, Network Configuration Operators, LocalGuest, Authenticated Users, LOCAL SERVICE, System Managed Accounts Group, NETWORK, Guests, CREATOR OWNER, Replicator, TERMINAL SERVER USER, WDAGUtilityAccount, BATCH, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DESKTOP-CRJIC3S\Derrick lattimore, Access Control Assistance Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Everyone, IIS_IUSRS, Distributed COM Users, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, PROXY, Power Users, Device Owners, CREATOR GROUP, Users, SYSTEM, LocalAdmin, Cryptographic Operators, Remote Desktop Users, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Hyper-V Administrators, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, IUSR, DefaultAccount, Remote Management Users, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, Performance Monitor Users
            Result Analysis:serestoreprivilege : Backup Operators = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

            V-257770 - Windows 11 must have command line process auditing events enabled for failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-257770r958412_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000585
            Identities:CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
            Description: When this policy setting is enabled, the operating system generates audit events when a process fails to start and the name of the program or user that created it.

            These audit events can assist in understanding how a computer is being used and tracking user activity.
            Check Text:Ensure Audit Process Creation auditing has been enabled:

            Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policy >> Detailed Tracking >> Audit Process Creation.

            If "Audit Process Creation" is not set to "Failure", this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Go to Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >>Security Settings>> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Detailed Tracking >> Set "Audit Process Creation" to "Failure".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:257770
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000585 - Windows 11 must have command line process auditing events enabled.
            Description:When this policy setting is enabled, the operating system generates audit events when a process is created (starts) and the name of the program or user that created it.

            These audit events can help with understanding how a computer is being used and tracking user activity.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Audit - Process Creation - Failure only)
                • false (Audit - Process Creation - Success and Failure)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25777000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Audit - Process Creation - Failure only
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25777000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment:Audit - Process Creation - Failure only
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'process_creation' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • application_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • computer_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • distribution_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • other_account_management_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • security_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • user_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • dpapi_activity equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • process_creation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • process_termination equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • rpc_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • directory_service_access equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • directory_service_changes equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
            • detailed_directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

            V-278926 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit file system failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278926r1135296_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000581
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Check Text:Verify that Audit File System auditing has been enabled:

            Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit File System.

            If "Audit File System" is not set to "Failure", this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit File System" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:278926
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000581 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit file system failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit File System' is set to 'Failure')
                • false ('Audit File System' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27894200 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Audit File System' is set to 'Failure'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:27894200 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
            State Comment:'File System' is set to 'Failure'
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'file_system' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'

            V-278927 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit file system successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278927r1135299_rule

            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Check Text:Verify that Audit File System auditing has been enabled:

            Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit File System.

            If "Audit File System" is not set to "Success", this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit File System" with "Success" selected.

            Weight:10.0

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
