            V-278928 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit handle manipulation failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278928r1135302_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000583
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Check Text:Verify that Audit Handle Manipulation auditing has been enabled:

            Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit Handle Manipulation.

            If "Audit Handle Manipulation" is not set to "Failure", this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Handle Manipulation" with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:278928
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000583 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit handle manipulation failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Failure')
                • false ('Audit Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27894400 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Audit Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Failure'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.

            State Comment:'Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Failure'
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'handle_manipulation' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'

Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.             V-278931 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit registry successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278931r1135311_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000586
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Check Text:Verify that Audit Registry auditing has been enabled:

            Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit Registry.

            If "Audit Registry" is not set to "Success", this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Registry" with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:278931
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000586 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit registry successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit Registry' is set to 'Success')
                • false ('Audit Registry' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27894700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Audit Registry' is set to 'Success'

            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • Collect any available items.

            V-278932 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit sensitive privilege use successes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278932r1141916_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000587
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Check Text:Verify that Audit Sensitive Privilege Use auditing has been enabled:

            Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Privilege Use >> Audit Sensitive Privilege Use.

            If "Audit Sensitive Privilege Use" is not set to "Success", this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Privilege Use >> Audit Sensitive Privilege Use with "Success" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:278932
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000587 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit sensitive privileged use successes.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))

                • false ('Audit Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')

            V-278933 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit sensitive privilege use failures.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278933r1141919_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-AU-000588
            Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
            Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Check Text:Verify that Audit Sensitive Privilege Use auditing has been enabled:

            Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Privilege Use >> Audit Sensitive Privilege Use.

            If "Audit Sensitive Privilege Use" is not set to "Failure", this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Privilege Use >> Audit Sensitive Privilege Use with "Failure" selected.
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:278933
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-AU-000588 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit sensitive privileged use failures.
            Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Audit Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Failure')
                • false ('Audit Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27894900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)

            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

            V-279688 - Windows 11 systems must block consumer account user authentication.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-279688r1153564_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-00-000126
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: It is detrimental for operating systems to provide, or install by default, functionality exceeding requirements or mission objectives. These unnecessary capabilities or services are often overlooked and therefore, may remain unsecured. They increase the risk to the platform by providing additional attack vectors.

            Operating systems are capable of providing a wide variety of functions and services. Some of the functions and services, provided by default, may not be necessary to support essential organizational operations (e.g., key missions, functions).

            The Copilot Rewrite functionality within Notepad and Image Generation within Paint is dependent upon the use of AI credits from a Microsoft 365 Personal or Family subscription. Organizational users must not use personal accounts to login to applications on enterprise machines.
            Check Text:Verify the "block all consumer Microsoft account user authentication" is enabled.

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\MicrosoftAccount

            Value Name: DisableUserAuth

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000001 (1)

            If the registry value is not "1", this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the following Group Policy:
            Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Microsoft Account "block all consumer Microsoft account user authentication" to "Enabled".

            For systems managed by Intune, apply the DOD Windows 11 STIG Settings Catalog (or equivalent Intune policy) found in the Intune policy package available on cyber.mil.
            Steps to create an Intune policy:
            1. Sign in to the Intune admin center >> Devices >> Configuration >> Create >> New Policy.
            2. Platform: Windows 10 and later. Profile type: Settings Catalog, then click "Create".
            3. Basics: Provide a Name and Description of the profile, then click "Next".
            4. Configuration settings: Click "+ Add settings" and search for consumer under the Settings picker. Under the Administrative Templates\Windows Components\Microsoft account category, check the box next to "Block all consumer Microsoft account user authentication". Click the Enabled radio button, then click "Next".
            5. Scope tags: (optional), then click "Next".
            6. Assignments: Assign the policy to Entra security groups that contain the target users or devices, then click "Next".
            7. Review + create: Review the deployment summary, then click "Create".
            Severity:medium
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:279688
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-00-000126 - Windows 11 systems must block consumer account user authentication.
            Description:It is detrimental for operating systems to provide, or install by default, functionality exceeding requirements or mission objectives. These unnecessary capabilities or services are often overlooked and therefore, may remain unsecured. They increase the risk to the platform by providing additional attack vectors.

            Operating systems are capable of providing a wide variety of functions and services. Some of the functions and services, provided by default, may not be necessary to support essential organizational operations (e.g., key missions, functions).

            The Copilot Rewrite functionality within Notepad and Image Generation within Paint is dependent upon the use of AI credits from a Microsoft 365 Personal or Family subscription. Organizational users must not use personal accounts to login to applications on enterprise machines.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Prevent enabling lock screen camera' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27968800 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Prevent enabling lock screen camera' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:27968800 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\MicrosoftAccount'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableUserAuth'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\MicrosoftAccount\DisableUserAuth'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          Detailed Results: Low Severity (CAT III)

            V-253296 - The Windows 11 time service must synchronize with an appropriate DOD time source.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253296r1051041_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-00-000260
            Identities:CCI-004923 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-45 (1) (a))
            CCI-001891 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-8 (1) (a))
            Description: The Windows Time Service controls time synchronization settings. Time synchronization is essential for authentication and auditing purposes. If the Windows Time Service is used, it must synchronize with a secure, authorized time source. Domain-joined systems are automatically configured to synchronize with domain controllers. If an NTP server is configured, it must synchronize with a secure, authorized time source.
            Check Text:Review the Windows time service configuration.

            Open an elevated "Command Prompt" (run as administrator).

            Enter "W32tm /query /configuration".

            Domain-joined systems (excluding the domain controller with the PDC emulator role):

            If the value for "Type" under "NTP Client" is not "NT5DS", this is a finding.
            Fix Text:Configure the system to synchronize time with an appropriate DOD time source.

            Domain-joined systems use NT5DS to synchronize time from other systems in the domain by default.

            If the system needs to be configured to an NTP server, configure the system to point to an authorized time server by setting the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Windows Time Service >> Time Providers >> "Configure Windows NTP Client" to "Enabled", and configure the "NtpServer" field to point to an appropriate DOD time server.

            The US Naval Observatory operates stratum 1 time servers, identified at https://www.cnmoc.usff.navy.mil/Our-Commands/United-States-Naval-Observatory/Precise-Time-Department/Network-Time-Protocol-NTP/. Time synchronization will occur through a hierarchy of time servers down to the local level. Clients and lower-level servers will synchronize with an authorized time server in the hierarchy.
            Severity:low
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253296
            Definition Result:false
            Title:The Windows 11 time service must synchronize with an appropriate DOD time source.
            Description: 
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Review the Windows time service configuration via PowerShell)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25329601 (shellcommand_test)
            Result:false
            Title:Review the Windows time service configuration via PowerShell
            Check Existence:One and only one collected item may exist.
            Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25329601 (shellcommand_object)
            Object Comment:Review the Windows time service configuration via PowerShell
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'shell' must be equal to 'powershell'
            • check = 'command' must be equal to 'W32tm /query /configuration'
            • check = 'pattern' must be equal to '^Type:'
            State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25329601 (shellcommand_state)
            State Comment:Confirm ExitStatus is 0 and stdout is NT5DS
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'command' must be equal to 'W32tm /query /configuration'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'pattern' must be equal to '^Type:'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'exit_status' must be equal to '0'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'stdout_line' must match the pattern 'NT5DS'
            Collected Item/State Result:
            [ false ]
            • shell equals 'powershell'
            • command equals 'W32tm /query /configuration'
            • pattern equals '^Type:'
            • exit_status equals '2147023834'
            Result Analysis:Failed state entity existence check. , Element: 'stdout_line' does not exist.

            V-253355 - The system must be configured to prevent Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) redirects from overriding Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) generated routes.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253355r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000030
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Allowing ICMP redirect of routes can lead to traffic not being routed properly. When disabled, this forces ICMP to be routed via shortest path first.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\

            Value Name: EnableICMPRedirect

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes" to "Disabled".

            This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
            Severity:low
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253355
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000030 - The system must be configured to prevent Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) redirects from overriding Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) generated routes.
            Description:Allowing ICMP redirect of routes can lead to traffic not being routed properly. When disabled, this forces ICMP to be routed via shortest path first.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes' is set to 'Disabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335500 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes' is set to 'Disabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335500 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnableICMPRedirect'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\EnableICMPRedirect'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253356 - The system must be configured to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253356r958902_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000035
            Identities:CCI-002385 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-5; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-5 a)
            Description: Configuring the system to ignore name release requests, except from WINS servers, prevents a denial of service (DoS) attack. The DoS consists of sending a NetBIOS name release request to the server for each entry in the server's cache, causing a response delay in the normal operation of the servers WINS resolution capability.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netbt\Parameters\

            Value Name: NoNameReleaseOnDemand

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (NoNameReleaseOnDemand) Allow the computer to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers" to "Enabled".

            This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
            Severity:low
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253356
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000035 - The system must be configured to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers.
            Description:Configuring the system to ignore name release requests, except from WINS servers, prevents a denial of service (DoS) attack. The DoS consists of sending a NetBIOS name release request to the server for each entry in the server's cache, causing a response delay in the normal operation of the servers WINS resolution capability.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))

            V-253384 - The setting to allow Microsoft accounts to be optional for modern style apps must be enabled.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253384r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000170
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Control of credentials and the system must be maintained within the enterprise. Enabling this setting allows enterprise credentials to be used with modern style apps that support this, instead of Microsoft accounts.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

            Value Name: MSAOptional

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000001 (1)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> App Runtime >> "Allow Microsoft accounts to be optional" to "Enabled".
            Severity:low
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11

            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Allow Microsoft accounts to be optional' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338400 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Allow Microsoft accounts to be optional' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338400 (registry_object)
            Object Comment:

            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'MSAOptional'

            V-253385 - The Application Compatibility Program Inventory must be prevented from collecting data and sending the information to Microsoft.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253385r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000175
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting will prevent the Program Inventory from collecting data about a system and sending the information to Microsoft.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat\

            Value Name: DisableInventory

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Application Compatibility >> "Turn off Inventory Collector" to "Enabled".
            Severity:low
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253385
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000175 - The Application Compatibility Program Inventory must be prevented from collecting data and sending the information to Microsoft.
            Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting will prevent the Program Inventory from collecting data about a system and sending the information to Microsoft.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Turn off Inventory Collector' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338500 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Turn off Inventory Collector' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338500 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableInventory'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat\DisableInventory'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

            V-253390 - Microsoft consumer experiences must be turned off.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253390r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000197
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: Microsoft consumer experiences provides suggestions and notifications to users, which may include the installation of Windows Store apps. Organizations may control the execution of applications through other means such as allowlisting. Turning off Microsoft consumer experiences will help prevent the unwanted installation of suggested applications.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent\

            Value Name: DisableWindowsConsumerFeatures

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000001 (1)
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Cloud Content >> "Turn off Microsoft consumer experiences" to "Enabled".

            Class:compliance

                • false ('Turn off Microsoft consumer experiences' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339000 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Turn off Microsoft consumer experiences' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.

            V-253394 - Windows Update must not obtain updates from other PCs on the internet.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253394r991589_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-CC-000206
            Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
            Description: Windows 11 allows Windows Update to obtain updates from additional sources instead of Microsoft. In addition to Microsoft, updates can be obtained from and sent to PCs on the local network as well as on the Internet. This is part of the Windows Update trusted process, however to minimize outside exposure, obtaining updates from or sending to systems on the internet must be prevented.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization\

            Value Name: DODownloadMode

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000000 (0) - No peering (HTTP Only)
            0x00000001 (1) - Peers on same NAT only (LAN)
            0x00000002 (2) - Local Network / Private group peering (Group)
            0x00000063 (99) - Simple download mode, no peering (Simple)
            0x00000064 (100) - Bypass mode, Delivery Optimization not used (Bypass)

            A value of 0x00000003 (3), Internet, is a finding.

            Standalone systems (configured in Settings):
            If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\DeliveryOptimization\Config\

            Value Name: DODownloadMode

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000000 (0) - Off
            0x00000001 (1) - LAN
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Delivery Optimization >> "Download Mode" to "Enabled" with any option except "Internet" selected.

            Acceptable selections include:
            Bypass (100)
            Group (2)
            HTTP only (0)
            LAN (1)
            Simple (99)

            .
            Severity:low
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253394
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-CC-000206 - Windows Update must not obtain updates from other PCs on the internet.
            Description:Windows 11 allows Windows Update to obtain updates from additional sources instead of Microsoft. In addition to Microsoft, updates can be obtained from and sent to PCs on the local network as well as on the Internet. This is part of the Windows Update trusted process, however to minimize outside exposure, obtaining updates from or sending to systems on the internet must be prevented.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • true (The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation or Server)
                  • false ('DownloadMode' is set to 'Enabled' with 'HTTP only or LAN' selected.)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false ('DownloadMode' is set to 'Enabled' with 'HTTP only, LAN, Group, Simple or Bypass' selected.)
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339402 (wmi57_test)
            Result:true
            Title:The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation or Server
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
            • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000100 (wmi57_state)
            State Comment:Standalone Workstation
            State Requirements:
            • for all 'result' the following must be true:
              • all domainrole must be equal to '0'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000300 (wmi57_state)
            State Comment:Standalone Server
            State Requirements:
            • for all 'result' the following must be true:
              • all domainrole must be equal to '2'
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339403 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'DownloadMode' is set to 'Enabled' with 'HTTP only or LAN' selected.
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339401 (registry_object)
            Object Comment:DownloadMode set through the Settings App
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\DeliveryOptimization\Config'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DODownloadMode'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\DeliveryOptimization\Config\DODownloadMode'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339401 (registry_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'DownloadMode' is set to 'Enabled' with 'HTTP only, LAN, Group, Simple or Bypass' selected.
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
            Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
            State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
            Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339400 (registry_object)
            Object Comment: 
            Object Requirements:
            • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DODownloadMode'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
            State Comment: 
            State Requirements:
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)
            State Comment:

            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
            • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2'
            State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25339400 (registry_state)

            V-253425 - Windows 11 must be configured to prevent users from receiving suggestions for third-party or additional applications.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253425r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail

            Description: Windows spotlight features may suggest apps and content from third-party software publishers in addition to Microsoft apps and content.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_CURRENT_USER

            Value Name: DisableThirdPartySuggestions

            Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 0x00000001 (1)

            V-253446 - The Windows message title for the legal notice must be configured.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253446r958586_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-SO-000080
            Identities:CCI-000048 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-8 a; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-8.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-8 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-8 a)
            CCI-001384 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-8 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-8.2 (i); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-8 c 1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-8 c 1)
            CCI-001385 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-8 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-8.2 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-8 c 2; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-8 c 2)
            CCI-001386 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-8 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-8.2 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-8 c 2; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-8 c 2)
            CCI-001387 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-8 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-8.2 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-8 c 2; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-8 c 2)
            CCI-001388 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-8 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-8.2 (iii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-8 c 3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-8 c 3)
            Description: Failure to display the logon banner prior to a logon attempt will negate legal proceedings resulting from unauthorized access to system resources.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

            Value Name: LegalNoticeCaption

            Value Type: REG_SZ
            Value: See message title above

            "DoD Notice and Consent Banner", "US Department of Defense Warning Statement" or a site-defined equivalent, this is a finding.

            If a site-defined title is used, it can in no case contravene or modify the language of the banner text required in WN11-SO-000075.
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Interactive logon: Message title for users attempting to log on" to "DoD Notice and Consent Banner", "US Department of Defense Warning Statement", or a site-defined equivalent.

            If a site-defined title is used, it can in no case contravene or modify the language of the banner text required in WN11-SO-000075.
            Severity:low
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253446
            Definition Result:false
            Title:The Windows message title for the legal notice must be configured.
            Description:Failure to display the logon banner prior to a logon attempt will negate legal proceedings resulting from unauthorized access to system resources.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (The Windows dialog box title for the legal banner must be configured. Title Option 1)
              • false (The Windows dialog box title for the legal banner must be configured. Title Option 2)

            Title:The Windows dialog box title for the legal banner must be configured. Title Option 1
            Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.

            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'

            V-253477 - Toast notifications to the lock screen must be turned off.

            Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253477r958478_rule
            Test Type:Automated
            Rule Result:Fail
            Version:WN11-UC-000015
            Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
            Description: Toast notifications that are displayed on the lock screen could display sensitive information to unauthorized personnel. Turning off this feature will limit access to the information to a logged on user.
            Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

            Registry Hive: HKEY_CURRENT_USER
            Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications\

            Value Name: NoToastApplicationNotificationOnLockScreen

            Value Type: REG_DWORD
            Value: 1
            Fix Text:Configure the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Start Menu and Taskbar >> Notifications >> "Turn off toast notifications on the lock screen" to "Enabled".
            Severity:low
            Weight:10.0
            Reference:
            Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
            Publisher:DISA
            Type:DPMS Target
            Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
            Identifier:5471
            Definitions:
            Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253477
            Definition Result:false
            Title:WN11-UC-000015 - Toast notifications to the lock screen must be turned off.
            Description:Toast notifications that are displayed on the lock screen could display sensitive information to unauthorized personnel. Turning off this feature will limit access to the information to a logged on user.
            Class:compliance
            Tests:
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('Turn off toast notifications on the lock screen' is set to 'Enabled')
                • false (For the current user, 'Turn off toast notifications on the lock screen' is set to 'Enabled')
            Tests:
            Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25347700 (ntuser_test)
            Result:false
            Title:'Turn off toast notifications on the lock screen' is set to 'Enabled'
            Check Existence:All collected items must exist.

            Object Comment:

            • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications'
            • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoToastApplicationNotificationOnLockScreen'

            • enabled equals 'false'
